title: A New Neighbour
summary: A woman describes meeting her new neighbour who recently moved in next door. She talks about their first conversation, what she learned about him, and how she helped him settle in.
body: Last month a new neighbour moved into the flat next to mine. His name is David and he comes from Scotland. I first met him on a Tuesday morning when I was leaving for work. He was standing in the hallway with some large boxes and looked a bit lost. I introduced myself and offered to help him carry the boxes inside. He was very grateful and invited me in for a cup of tea afterwards. Over tea we had a lovely conversation. David told me he moved here because he got a new job at a hospital in our area. He is a nurse and works long shifts. He also mentioned that he does not know anyone in the city yet, so I offered to show him around the neighbourhood. The following Saturday we walked to the local market together and I showed him the nearest supermarket, the pharmacy, and the post office. He said it was very helpful. David seems like a very kind and interesting person. I am glad he is my neighbour because our building felt quite empty before he arrived.

annotations.exampleEn: The new family moved in last week and we haven't met them yet.

annotations.meaningVi: căn hộ (cách nói của người Anh)
annotations.exampleEn: She rents a flat on the third floor of the building.

annotations.meaningVi: hành lang, lối đi trong nhà
annotations.exampleEn: He left his umbrella in the hallway by the door.

annotations.meaningVi: trông có vẻ bối rối, không biết phải làm gì
annotations.exampleEn: She looked a bit lost so I offered to help her find the office.
He walked up to the group and introduced himself confidently.
She was very grateful for all the help her friends gave her.
Nurses often work long shifts of twelve hours or more.

annotations.meaningVi: khu phố, khu vực xung quanh nơi ở
annotations.exampleEn: It is a quiet neighbourhood with a park and good schools nearby.

annotations.meaningVi: dẫn ai đó đi tham quan, chỉ dẫn nơi chốn
annotations.exampleEn: She offered to show the new student around the school.
I need to go to the pharmacy to pick up my prescription.

annotations.meaningVi: ổn định trong môi trường mới, làm quen với nơi mới
annotations.exampleEn: It took him a few weeks to settle in at his new school.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for sequential past events: 'I met', 'He moved', 'I offered'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong đoạn văn này, thì quá khứ đơn được dùng để kể lại một chuỗi sự kiện đã xảy ra theo thứ tự. 'Last month a new neighbour moved in.' → 'I first met him on a Tuesday morning.' → 'I introduced myself and offered to help.' → 'He invited me in for a cup of tea.' Mỗi hành động xảy ra sau hành động trước đó. Hãy chú ý các từ chỉ thời gian: last month, on a Tuesday morning, afterwards, the following Saturday.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The simple past tense is used to tell a story about events in order. Time markers help sequence the events: 'Last month' (when he moved in) → 'on a Tuesday morning' (when they first met) → 'afterwards' (after helping with boxes) → 'the following Saturday' (the next step). Each event is complete. Key irregular past forms in this passage: move → moved, meet → met, invite → invited, tell → told, feel → felt.

grammarNotes.title: Offering help: 'I offered to + infinitive'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Offer to + động từ nguyên thể' diễn tả việc đề nghị làm điều gì đó cho người khác. Ví dụ: 'I offered to help him carry the boxes.' — Tôi đề nghị giúp anh ấy mang hộp. 'I offered to show him around the neighbourhood.' — Tôi đề nghị dẫn anh ấy đi tham quan khu phố. Cấu trúc này thể hiện sự tốt bụng và chủ động giúp đỡ người khác.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'offer + to + infinitive' means to volunteer to do something for someone else. Examples: 'I offered to help him carry the boxes.' / 'I offered to show him around the neighbourhood.' This is different from 'suggest', which uses -ing: 'I suggested walking to the market.' Compare also: 'He invited me in' (invited = asked me to come in) versus 'He offered me tea' (offered = gave or made available).

translationVi: Tháng trước một người hàng xóm mới dọn vào căn hộ kế bên tôi. Tên anh ấy là David và anh ấy đến từ Scotland. Tôi gặp anh ấy lần đầu vào sáng thứ ba khi tôi đang rời nhà đi làm. Anh ấy đứng trong hành lang với vài chiếc hộp lớn và trông có vẻ bối rối. Tôi tự giới thiệu bản thân và đề nghị giúp anh ấy mang hộp vào trong. Anh ấy rất biết ơn và mời tôi vào uống trà. Trong khi uống trà chúng tôi trò chuyện rất vui. David kể anh ấy chuyển đến đây vì nhận được công việc mới tại bệnh viện trong khu vực. Anh ấy là y tá và làm ca dài. Anh ấy cũng đề cập rằng chưa quen biết ai trong thành phố, vì vậy tôi đề nghị dẫn anh ấy đi tham quan khu phố. Thứ bảy tuần sau chúng tôi đi bộ đến chợ địa phương cùng nhau và tôi chỉ cho anh ấy siêu thị gần nhất, nhà thuốc và bưu điện. Anh ấy nói rằng rất hữu ích. David có vẻ là một người rất tốt bụng và thú vị. Tôi vui vì anh ấy là hàng xóm của tôi vì tòa nhà của chúng tôi cảm thấy khá vắng vẻ trước khi anh ấy đến.

questions.prompt: When did the new neighbour move in?
questions.explanation: 'Last month a new neighbour moved into the flat next to mine.'
questions.hint: The first sentence gives the time.

questions.explanation: 'His name is David and he comes from Scotland.'
questions.hint: His home country is stated in the second sentence.

questions.prompt: When did the narrator first meet David?
questions.explanation: 'I first met him on a Tuesday morning when I was leaving for work.'
questions.hint: The day and time of day are both mentioned.

questions.prompt: What was David doing when she first saw him?
options: He was knocking on her door
He was unpacking in his flat
He was standing in the hallway with large boxes
He was talking on the phone
questions.explanation: 'He was standing in the hallway with some large boxes and looked a bit lost.'
questions.hint: He was in a shared space with his belongings.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator offer to do?
options: Help him carry the boxes inside
questions.explanation: 'I offered to help him carry the boxes inside.'
questions.hint: Her offer was directly related to what he was doing.

questions.prompt: Why did David move to the city?
options: He wanted to study at university
He got a new job at a hospital
He was looking for a cheaper flat
questions.explanation: 'David told me he moved here because he got a new job at a hospital in our area.'
questions.hint: He gives a clear reason for moving.

questions.explanation: 'He is a nurse and works long shifts.'
questions.hint: His job is stated directly in the passage.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator offer to do for David?
options: Help him find a new job
questions.explanation: 'I offered to show him around the neighbourhood.'
questions.hint: She offered because he didn't know anyone in the city.

questions.prompt: When did they walk around the neighbourhood together?
questions.explanation: 'The following Saturday we walked to the local market together.'
questions.hint: The time is expressed with 'the following'.

questions.prompt: Which of these places did she NOT show him?
questions.explanation: She showed him the supermarket, pharmacy, and post office — not the library.
questions.hint: Three places are listed; identify the one not mentioned.

questions.prompt: How did David react to the neighbourhood tour?
options: He said it was too short
He said it was very helpful
He said he knew the area already

questions.prompt: Why was the narrator glad about David moving in?
options: Because she was lonely and the building felt empty
Because he helps her with heavy things
Because she needed a friend at work
questions.explanation: 'I am glad he is my neighbour because our building felt quite empty before he arrived.'
questions.hint: She gives a reason connected to the atmosphere of the building.

blanks.explanation: 'neighbour' — someone who lives next door or nearby.
'Scotland' — the passage states David comes from Scotland.
'hallway' — the hallway is the shared corridor area inside a building.
'grateful' — he felt thankful for her help with the boxes.
'tea' — he invited her in for a cup of tea.
'hospital' — David got a new job at a hospital.
'shifts' — nurses work in shifts, meaning set periods of time on duty.
'neighbourhood' — the local area around where they live.
'Saturday' — 'the following Saturday' means the next Saturday after that.
'helpful' — David said the tour was very helpful.
'kind' — the narrator describes David as kind and interesting.
'empty' — the building felt quite empty before David arrived.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator helped her new neighbour settle into the area. In many modern cities, people do not know their neighbours well. Why do you think this happens, and what could people do to build better relationships with those who live near them?
